description:
Network domain allows tracking network activities of the page. It exposes information about http,
file, data and other requests and responses, their headers, bodies, timing, etc.

types.description: Resource type as it was perceived by the rendering engine.
Unique loader identifier.

Unique network request identifier.
Note that this does not identify individual HTTP requests that are part of
a network request.
Unique intercepted request identifier.
Network level fetch failure reason.
UTC time in seconds, counted from January 1, 1970.
Monotonically increasing time in seconds since an arbitrary point in the past.
Request / response headers as keys / values of JSON object.
The underlying connection technology that the browser is supposedly using.

Represents the cookie's 'SameSite' status:
https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-west-first-party-cookies

Represents the cookie's 'Priority' status:
https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-west-cookie-priority-00

Represents the source scheme of the origin that originally set the cookie.
A value of "Unset" allows protocol clients to emulate legacy cookie scope for the scheme.
This is a temporary ability and it will be removed in the future.
Timing information for the request.

properties.name: requestTime
properties.description:
Timing's requestTime is a baseline in seconds, while the other numbers are ticks in
milliseconds relatively to this requestTime.

properties.name: proxyStart
properties.description: Started resolving proxy.

properties.name: proxyEnd
properties.description: Finished resolving proxy.

properties.name: dnsStart
properties.description: Started DNS address resolve.

properties.name: dnsEnd
properties.description: Finished DNS address resolve.

properties.name: connectStart
properties.description: Started connecting to the remote host.

properties.name: connectEnd
properties.description: Connected to the remote host.

properties.name: sslStart
properties.description: Started SSL handshake.

properties.name: sslEnd
properties.description: Finished SSL handshake.

properties.name: workerStart
properties.description: Started running ServiceWorker.

properties.name: workerReady
properties.description: Finished Starting ServiceWorker.

properties.name: workerFetchStart
properties.description: Started fetch event.

properties.name: workerRespondWithSettled
properties.description: Settled fetch event respondWith promise.

properties.name: workerRouterEvaluationStart
properties.description: Started ServiceWorker static routing source evaluation.

properties.name: workerCacheLookupStart
properties.description: Started cache lookup when the source was evaluated to `cache`.

properties.name: sendStart
properties.description: Started sending request.

properties.name: sendEnd
properties.description: Finished sending request.

properties.name: pushStart
properties.description: Time the server started pushing request.

properties.name: pushEnd
properties.description: Time the server finished pushing request.

properties.name: receiveHeadersStart
properties.description: Started receiving response headers.

properties.name: receiveHeadersEnd
properties.description: Finished receiving response headers.

types.description: Loading priority of a resource request.
The render-blocking behavior of a resource request.
Post data entry for HTTP request
HTTP request data.

properties.name: url
properties.description: Request URL (without fragment).

properties.name: urlFragment
properties.description: Fragment of the requested URL starting with hash, if present.

properties.name: method
properties.description: HTTP request method.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: HTTP request headers.

properties.name: postData
properties.description:
HTTP POST request data.
Use postDataEntries instead.

properties.name: hasPostData
properties.description: True when the request has POST data. Note that postData might still be omitted when this flag is true when the data is too long.

properties.name: postDataEntries
properties.description: Request body elements (post data broken into individual entries).

properties.name: mixedContentType
properties.description: The mixed content type of the request.

properties.name: initialPriority
properties.description: Priority of the resource request at the time request is sent.

properties.name: referrerPolicy
properties.description: The referrer policy of the request, as defined in https://www.w3.org/TR/referrer-policy/

properties.name: isLinkPreload
properties.description: Whether is loaded via link preload.

properties.name: trustTokenParams
properties.description:
Set for requests when the TrustToken API is used. Contains the parameters
passed by the developer (e.g. via "fetch") as understood by the backend.

properties.name: isSameSite
properties.description:
True if this resource request is considered to be the 'same site' as the
request corresponding to the main frame.

properties.name: isAdRelated
properties.description: True when the resource request is ad-related.

types.description: Details of a signed certificate timestamp (SCT).

properties.name: status
properties.description: Validation status.

properties.name: origin
properties.description: Origin.

properties.name: logDescription
properties.description: Log name / description.

properties.name: logId
properties.description: Log ID.

properties.name: timestamp
properties.description:
Issuance date. Unlike TimeSinceEpoch, this contains the number of
milliseconds since January 1, 1970, UTC, not the number of seconds.

properties.name: hashAlgorithm
properties.description: Hash algorithm.

properties.name: signatureAlgorithm
properties.description: Signature algorithm.

properties.name: signatureData
properties.description: Signature data.

types.description: Security details about a request.

properties.name: protocol
properties.description: Protocol name (e.g. "TLS 1.2" or "QUIC").

properties.name: keyExchange
properties.description: Key Exchange used by the connection, or the empty string if not applicable.

properties.name: keyExchangeGroup
properties.description: (EC)DH group used by the connection, if applicable.

properties.name: cipher
properties.description: Cipher name.

properties.name: mac
properties.description: TLS MAC. Note that AEAD ciphers do not have separate MACs.

properties.name: certificateId
properties.description: Certificate ID value.

properties.name: subjectName
properties.description: Certificate subject name.

properties.name: sanList
properties.description: Subject Alternative Name (SAN) DNS names and IP addresses.

properties.name: issuer
properties.description: Name of the issuing CA.

properties.name: validFrom
properties.description: Certificate valid from date.

properties.name: validTo
properties.description: Certificate valid to (expiration) date

properties.name: signedCertificateTimestampList
properties.description: List of signed certificate timestamps (SCTs).

properties.name: certificateTransparencyCompliance
properties.description: Whether the request complied with Certificate Transparency policy

properties.name: serverSignatureAlgorithm
properties.description:
The signature algorithm used by the server in the TLS server signature,
represented as a TLS SignatureScheme code point. Omitted if not
applicable or not known.

properties.name: encryptedClientHello
properties.description: Whether the connection used Encrypted ClientHello

types.description: Whether the request complied with Certificate Transparency policy.
The reason why request was blocked.
The reason why request was blocked.
Source of serviceworker response.

Determines what type of Trust Token operation is executed and
depending on the type, some additional parameters. The values
are specified in third_party/blink/renderer/core/fetch/trust_token.idl.

properties.name: refreshPolicy
properties.description:
Only set for "token-redemption" operation and determine whether
to request a fresh SRR or use a still valid cached SRR.

properties.name: issuers
properties.description:
Origins of issuers from whom to request tokens or redemption
records.

types.description: The reason why Chrome uses a specific transport protocol for HTTP semantics.
Source of service worker router.

properties.name: ruleIdMatched
properties.description:
ID of the rule matched. If there is a matched rule, this field will
be set, otherwiser no value will be set.

properties.name: matchedSourceType
properties.description:
The router source of the matched rule. If there is a matched rule, this
field will be set, otherwise no value will be set.

properties.name: actualSourceType
properties.description: The actual router source used.

types.description: HTTP response data.

properties.name: url
properties.description: Response URL. This URL can be different from CachedResource.url in case of redirect.

properties.name: status
properties.description: HTTP response status code.

properties.name: statusText
properties.description: HTTP response status text.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: HTTP response headers.

properties.name: headersText
properties.description: HTTP response headers text. This has been replaced by the headers in Network.responseReceivedExtraInfo.

properties.name: mimeType
properties.description: Resource mimeType as determined by the browser.

properties.name: charset
properties.description: Resource charset as determined by the browser (if applicable).

properties.name: requestHeaders
properties.description: Refined HTTP request headers that were actually transmitted over the network.

properties.name: requestHeadersText
properties.description: HTTP request headers text. This has been replaced by the headers in Network.requestWillBeSentExtraInfo.

properties.name: connectionReused
properties.description: Specifies whether physical connection was actually reused for this request.

properties.name: connectionId
properties.description: Physical connection id that was actually used for this request.

properties.name: remoteIPAddress
properties.description: Remote IP address.

properties.name: remotePort
properties.description: Remote port.

properties.name: fromDiskCache
properties.description: Specifies that the request was served from the disk cache.

properties.name: fromServiceWorker
properties.description: Specifies that the request was served from the ServiceWorker.

properties.name: fromPrefetchCache
properties.description: Specifies that the request was served from the prefetch cache.

properties.name: fromEarlyHints
properties.description: Specifies that the request was served from the prefetch cache.

properties.name: serviceWorkerRouterInfo
properties.description:
Information about how ServiceWorker Static Router API was used. If this
field is set with `matchedSourceType` field, a matching rule is found.
If this field is set without `matchedSource`, no matching rule is found.
Otherwise, the API is not used.

properties.name: encodedDataLength
properties.description: Total number of bytes received for this request so far.

properties.name: timing
properties.description: Timing information for the given request.

properties.name: serviceWorkerResponseSource
properties.description: Response source of response from ServiceWorker.

properties.name: responseTime
properties.description: The time at which the returned response was generated.

properties.name: cacheStorageCacheName
properties.description: Cache Storage Cache Name.

properties.name: protocol
properties.description: Protocol used to fetch this request.

properties.name: alternateProtocolUsage
properties.description: The reason why Chrome uses a specific transport protocol for HTTP semantics.

properties.name: securityState
properties.description: Security state of the request resource.

properties.name: securityDetails
properties.description: Security details for the request.

types.description: WebSocket request data.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: HTTP request headers.

types.description: WebSocket response data.

properties.name: status
properties.description: HTTP response status code.

properties.name: statusText
properties.description: HTTP response status text.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: HTTP response headers.

properties.name: headersText
properties.description: HTTP response headers text.

properties.name: requestHeaders
properties.description: HTTP request headers.

properties.name: requestHeadersText
properties.description: HTTP request headers text.

types.description: WebSocket message data. This represents an entire WebSocket message, not just a fragmented frame as the name suggests.

properties.name: opcode
properties.description: WebSocket message opcode.

properties.name: mask
properties.description: WebSocket message mask.

properties.name: payloadData
properties.description:
WebSocket message payload data.
If the opcode is 1, this is a text message and payloadData is a UTF-8 string.
If the opcode isn't 1, then payloadData is a base64 encoded string representing binary data.

types.description: Information about the cached resource.

properties.name: url
properties.description: Resource URL. This is the url of the original network request.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Type of this resource.

properties.name: response
properties.description: Cached response data.

properties.name: bodySize
properties.description: Cached response body size.

types.description: Information about the request initiator.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Type of this initiator.

properties.name: stack
properties.description:
Initiator JavaScript stack trace, set for Script only.
Requires the Debugger domain to be enabled.

properties.name: url
properties.description: Initiator URL, set for Parser type or for Script type (when script is importing module) or for SignedExchange type.

properties.name: lineNumber
properties.description:
Initiator line number, set for Parser type or for Script type (when script is importing
module) (0-based).

properties.name: columnNumber
properties.description:
Initiator column number, set for Parser type or for Script type (when script is importing
module) (0-based).

properties.name: requestId
properties.description: Set if another request triggered this request (e.g. preflight).

types.description:
cookiePartitionKey object
The representation of the components of the key that are created by the cookiePartitionKey class contained in net/cookies/cookie_partition_key.h.

properties.name: topLevelSite
properties.description:
The site of the top-level URL the browser was visiting at the start
of the request to the endpoint that set the cookie.

properties.name: hasCrossSiteAncestor
properties.description: Indicates if the cookie has any ancestors that are cross-site to the topLevelSite.

types.description: Cookie object

properties.name: name
properties.description: Cookie name.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Cookie value.

properties.name: domain
properties.description: Cookie domain.

properties.name: path
properties.description: Cookie path.

properties.name: expires
properties.description:
Cookie expiration date as the number of seconds since the UNIX epoch.
The value is set to -1 if the expiry date is not set.
The value can be null for values that cannot be represented in
JSON (±Inf).

properties.name: size
properties.description: Cookie size.

properties.name: httpOnly
properties.description: True if cookie is http-only.

properties.name: secure
properties.description: True if cookie is secure.

properties.name: session
properties.description: True in case of session cookie.

properties.name: sameSite
properties.description: Cookie SameSite type.

properties.name: priority
properties.description: Cookie Priority

properties.name: sourceScheme
properties.description: Cookie source scheme type.

properties.name: sourcePort
properties.description:
Cookie source port. Valid values are {-1, [1, 65535]}, -1 indicates an unspecified port.
An unspecified port value allows protocol clients to emulate legacy cookie scope for the port.
This is a temporary ability and it will be removed in the future.

properties.name: partitionKey
properties.description: Cookie partition key.

properties.name: partitionKeyOpaque
properties.description: True if cookie partition key is opaque.

types.description: Types of reasons why a cookie may not be stored from a response.
Types of reasons why a cookie may not be sent with a request.
Types of reasons why a cookie should have been blocked by 3PCD but is exempted for the request.
A cookie which was not stored from a response with the corresponding reason.

properties.name: blockedReasons
properties.description: The reason(s) this cookie was blocked.

properties.name: cookieLine
properties.description:
The string representing this individual cookie as it would appear in the header.
This is not the entire "cookie" or "set-cookie" header which could have multiple cookies.

properties.name: cookie
properties.description:
The cookie object which represents the cookie which was not stored. It is optional because
sometimes complete cookie information is not available, such as in the case of parsing
errors.

types.description:
A cookie should have been blocked by 3PCD but is exempted and stored from a response with the
corresponding reason. A cookie could only have at most one exemption reason.

properties.name: exemptionReason
properties.description: The reason the cookie was exempted.

properties.name: cookieLine
properties.description: The string representing this individual cookie as it would appear in the header.

properties.name: cookie
properties.description: The cookie object representing the cookie.

types.description:
A cookie associated with the request which may or may not be sent with it.
Includes the cookies itself and reasons for blocking or exemption.

properties.name: cookie
properties.description: The cookie object representing the cookie which was not sent.

properties.name: blockedReasons
properties.description: The reason(s) the cookie was blocked. If empty means the cookie is included.

properties.name: exemptionReason
properties.description:
The reason the cookie should have been blocked by 3PCD but is exempted. A cookie could
only have at most one exemption reason.

types.description: Cookie parameter object

properties.name: name
properties.description: Cookie name.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Cookie value.

properties.name: url
properties.description:
The request-URI to associate with the setting of the cookie. This value can affect the
default domain, path, source port, and source scheme values of the created cookie.

properties.name: domain
properties.description: Cookie domain.

properties.name: path
properties.description: Cookie path.

properties.name: secure
properties.description: True if cookie is secure.

properties.name: httpOnly
properties.description: True if cookie is http-only.

properties.name: sameSite
properties.description: Cookie SameSite type.

properties.name: expires
properties.description: Cookie expiration date, session cookie if not set

properties.name: priority
properties.description: Cookie Priority.

properties.name: sourceScheme
properties.description: Cookie source scheme type.

properties.name: sourcePort
properties.description:
Cookie source port. Valid values are {-1, [1, 65535]}, -1 indicates an unspecified port.
An unspecified port value allows protocol clients to emulate legacy cookie scope for the port.
This is a temporary ability and it will be removed in the future.

properties.name: partitionKey
properties.description: Cookie partition key. If not set, the cookie will be set as not partitioned.

types.description: Authorization challenge for HTTP status code 401 or 407.

properties.name: source
properties.description: Source of the authentication challenge.

properties.name: origin
properties.description: Origin of the challenger.

properties.name: scheme
properties.description: The authentication scheme used, such as basic or digest

properties.name: realm
properties.description: The realm of the challenge. May be empty.

types.description: Response to an AuthChallenge.

properties.name: response
properties.description:
The decision on what to do in response to the authorization challenge.  Default means
deferring to the default behavior of the net stack, which will likely either the Cancel
authentication or display a popup dialog box.

properties.name: username
properties.description:
The username to provide, possibly empty. Should only be set if response is
ProvideCredentials.

properties.name: password
properties.description:
The password to provide, possibly empty. Should only be set if response is
ProvideCredentials.

types.description:
Stages of the interception to begin intercepting. Request will intercept before the request is
sent. Response will intercept after the response is received.
Request pattern for interception.

properties.name: urlPattern
properties.description:
Wildcards (`'*'` -> zero or more, `'?'` -> exactly one) are allowed. Escape character is
backslash. Omitting is equivalent to `"*"`.

properties.name: resourceType
properties.description: If set, only requests for matching resource types will be intercepted.

properties.name: interceptionStage
properties.description: Stage at which to begin intercepting requests. Default is Request.

types.description:
Information about a signed exchange signature.
https://wicg.github.io/webpackage/draft-yasskin-httpbis-origin-signed-exchanges-impl.html#rfc.section.3.1

properties.name: label
properties.description: Signed exchange signature label.

properties.name: signature
properties.description: The hex string of signed exchange signature.

properties.name: integrity
properties.description: Signed exchange signature integrity.

properties.name: certUrl
properties.description: Signed exchange signature cert Url.

properties.name: certSha256
properties.description: The hex string of signed exchange signature cert sha256.

properties.name: validityUrl
properties.description: Signed exchange signature validity Url.

properties.name: date
properties.description: Signed exchange signature date.

properties.name: expires
properties.description: Signed exchange signature expires.

properties.name: certificates
properties.description: The encoded certificates.

types.description:
Information about a signed exchange header.
https://wicg.github.io/webpackage/draft-yasskin-httpbis-origin-signed-exchanges-impl.html#cbor-representation

properties.name: requestUrl
properties.description: Signed exchange request URL.

properties.name: responseCode
properties.description: Signed exchange response code.

properties.name: responseHeaders
properties.description: Signed exchange response headers.

properties.name: signatures
properties.description: Signed exchange response signature.

properties.name: headerIntegrity
properties.description: Signed exchange header integrity hash in the form of `sha256-<base64-hash-value>`.

types.description: Field type for a signed exchange related error.
Information about a signed exchange response.

properties.name: message
properties.description: Error message.

properties.name: signatureIndex
properties.description: The index of the signature which caused the error.

properties.name: errorField
properties.description: The field which caused the error.

types.description: Information about a signed exchange response.

properties.name: outerResponse
properties.description: The outer response of signed HTTP exchange which was received from network.

properties.name: hasExtraInfo
properties.description:
Whether network response for the signed exchange was accompanied by
extra headers.

properties.name: header
properties.description: Information about the signed exchange header.

properties.name: securityDetails
properties.description: Security details for the signed exchange header.

properties.name: errors
properties.description: Errors occurred while handling the signed exchange.

types.description: List of content encodings supported by the backend.

properties.name: urlPattern
properties.description:
Only matching requests will be affected by these conditions. Patterns use the URLPattern constructor string
syntax (https://urlpattern.spec.whatwg.org/) and must be absolute. If the pattern is empty, all requests are
matched (including p2p connections).

properties.name: latency
properties.description: Minimum latency from request sent to response headers received (ms).

properties.name: downloadThroughput
properties.description: Maximal aggregated download throughput (bytes/sec). -1 disables download throttling.

properties.name: uploadThroughput
properties.description: Maximal aggregated upload throughput (bytes/sec).  -1 disables upload throttling.

properties.name: connectionType
properties.description: Connection type if known.

properties.name: packetLoss
properties.description: WebRTC packet loss (percent, 0-100). 0 disables packet loss emulation, 100 drops all the packets.

properties.name: packetQueueLength
properties.description: WebRTC packet queue length (packet). 0 removes any queue length limitations.

properties.name: packetReordering
properties.description: WebRTC packetReordering feature.

properties.name: urlPattern
properties.description:
URL pattern to match. Patterns use the URLPattern constructor string syntax
(https://urlpattern.spec.whatwg.org/) and must be absolute. Example: `*://*:*/*.css`.

properties.name: block
properties.description:
Whether or not to block the pattern. If false, a matching request will not be blocked even if it matches a later
`BlockPattern`.

properties.name: noDelay
properties.description: TCP_NODELAY option

properties.name: keepAliveDelay
properties.description: Expected to be unsigned integer.

properties.name: sendBufferSize
properties.description: Expected to be unsigned integer.

properties.name: receiveBufferSize
properties.description: Expected to be unsigned integer.

properties.name: remotePort
properties.description: Unsigned int 16.

properties.name: localPort
properties.description: Unsigned int 16.

properties.name: sendBufferSize
properties.description: Expected to be unsigned integer.

properties.name: receiveBufferSize
properties.description: Expected to be unsigned integer.

properties.name: multicastTimeToLive
properties.description: Unsigned int 8.

properties.name: remoteAddr
properties.description: Null for connected mode.

properties.name: remotePort
properties.description:
Null for connected mode.
Expected to be unsigned integer.

properties.name: requestTime
properties.description:
Timing's requestTime is a baseline in seconds, while the other numbers are ticks in
milliseconds relatively to this requestTime. Matches ResourceTiming's requestTime for
the same request (but not for redirected requests).

types.description:
Identifies the script on the stack that caused a resource or element to be
labeled as an ad. For resources, this indicates the context that triggered
the fetch. For elements, this indicates the context that caused the element
to be appended to the DOM.

properties.name: scriptId
properties.description: The script's V8 identifier.

properties.name: debuggerId
properties.description: V8's debugging ID for the v8::Context.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The script's url (or generated name based on id if inline script).

types.description:
Encapsulates the script ancestry and the root script filter list rule that
caused the resource or element to be labeled as an ad.

properties.name: ancestryChain
properties.description:
A chain of `AdScriptIdentifier`s representing the ancestry of an ad
script that led to the creation of a resource or element. The chain is
ordered from the script itself (lowest level) up to its root ancestor
that was flagged by a filter list.

properties.name: rootScriptFilterlistRule
properties.description:
The filter list rule that caused the root (last) script in
`ancestryChain` to be tagged as an ad.

types.description:
Represents the provenance of an ad resource or element. Only one of
`filterlistRule` or `adScriptAncestry` can be set. If `filterlistRule`
is provided, the resource URL directly matches a filter list rule. If
`adScriptAncestry` is provided, an ad script initiated the resource fetch or
appended the element to the DOM. If neither is provided, the entity is
known to be an ad, but provenance tracking information is unavailable.

properties.name: filterlistRule
properties.description: The filterlist rule that matched, if any.

properties.name: adScriptAncestry
properties.description: The script ancestry that created the ad, if any.

types.description: The status of a Reporting API report.
An object representing a report generated by the Reporting API.

properties.name: initiatorUrl
properties.description: The URL of the document that triggered the report.

properties.name: destination
properties.description: The name of the endpoint group that should be used to deliver the report.

properties.name: type
properties.description: The type of the report (specifies the set of data that is contained in the report body).

properties.name: timestamp
properties.description: When the report was generated.

properties.name: depth
properties.description: How many uploads deep the related request was.

properties.name: completedAttempts
properties.description: The number of delivery attempts made so far, not including an active attempt.

properties.name: url
properties.description: The URL of the endpoint to which reports may be delivered.

properties.name: groupName
properties.description: Name of the endpoint group.

types.description: Unique identifier for a device bound session.

properties.name: site
properties.description: The site the session is set up for.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The id of the session.

types.description: How a device bound session was used during a request.

properties.name: sessionKey
properties.description: The key for the session.

properties.name: usage
properties.description: How the session was used (or not used).

types.description: A device bound session's cookie craving.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the craving.

properties.name: domain
properties.description: The domain of the craving.

properties.name: path
properties.description: The path of the craving.

properties.name: secure
properties.description: The `Secure` attribute of the craving attributes.

properties.name: httpOnly
properties.description: The `HttpOnly` attribute of the craving attributes.

properties.name: sameSite
properties.description: The `SameSite` attribute of the craving attributes.

types.description: A device bound session's inclusion URL rule.

properties.name: ruleType
properties.description: See comments on `net::device_bound_sessions::SessionInclusionRules::UrlRule::rule_type`.

properties.name: hostPattern
properties.description: See comments on `net::device_bound_sessions::SessionInclusionRules::UrlRule::host_pattern`.

properties.name: pathPrefix
properties.description: See comments on `net::device_bound_sessions::SessionInclusionRules::UrlRule::path_prefix`.

types.description: A device bound session's inclusion rules.

properties.name: origin
properties.description: See comments on `net::device_bound_sessions::SessionInclusionRules::origin_`.

properties.name: includeSite
properties.description:
Whether the whole site is included. See comments on
`net::device_bound_sessions::SessionInclusionRules::include_site_` for more
details; this boolean is true if that value is populated.

properties.name: urlRules
properties.description: See comments on `net::device_bound_sessions::SessionInclusionRules::url_rules_`.

types.description: A device bound session.

properties.name: key
properties.description: The site and session ID of the session.

properties.name: refreshUrl
properties.description: See comments on `net::device_bound_sessions::Session::refresh_url_`.

properties.name: inclusionRules
properties.description: See comments on `net::device_bound_sessions::Session::inclusion_rules_`.

properties.name: cookieCravings
properties.description: See comments on `net::device_bound_sessions::Session::cookie_cravings_`.

properties.name: expiryDate
properties.description: See comments on `net::device_bound_sessions::Session::expiry_date_`.

properties.name: cachedChallenge
properties.description: See comments on `net::device_bound_sessions::Session::cached_challenge__`.

properties.name: allowedRefreshInitiators
properties.description: See comments on `net::device_bound_sessions::Session::allowed_refresh_initiators_`.

types.description: A unique identifier for a device bound session event.
A fetch result for a device bound session creation or refresh.
Details about a failed device bound session network request.

properties.name: requestUrl
properties.description: The failed request URL.

properties.name: netError
properties.description: The net error of the response if it was not OK.

properties.name: responseError
properties.description:
The response code if the net error was OK and the response code was not
200.

properties.name: responseErrorBody
properties.description:
The body of the response if the net error was OK, the response code was
not 200, and the response body was not empty.

types.description: Session event details specific to creation.

properties.name: fetchResult
properties.description: The result of the fetch attempt.

properties.name: newSession
properties.description:
The session if there was a newly created session. This is populated for
all successful creation events.

properties.name: failedRequest
properties.description:
Details about a failed device bound session network request if there was
one.

types.description: Session event details specific to refresh.

properties.name: refreshResult
properties.description: The result of a refresh.

properties.name: fetchResult
properties.description: If there was a fetch attempt, the result of that.

properties.name: newSession
properties.description:
The session display if there was a newly created session. This is populated
for any refresh event that modifies the session config.

properties.name: wasFullyProactiveRefresh
properties.description: See comments on `net::device_bound_sessions::RefreshEventResult::was_fully_proactive_refresh`.

properties.name: failedRequest
properties.description:
Details about a failed device bound session network request if there was
one.

types.description: Session event details specific to termination.

properties.name: deletionReason
properties.description: The reason for a session being deleted.

types.description: Session event details specific to challenges.

properties.name: challengeResult
properties.description: The result of a challenge.

properties.name: challenge
properties.description: The challenge set.

types.description: An object providing the result of a network resource load.

properties.name: netError
properties.description: Optional values used for error reporting.

properties.name: stream
properties.description: If successful, one of the following two fields holds the result.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: Response headers.

types.description:
An options object that may be extended later to better support CORS,
CORB and streaming.

commands.name: setAcceptedEncodings
commands.description: Sets a list of content encodings that will be accepted. Empty list means no encoding is accepted.

parameters.name: encodings
parameters.description: List of accepted content encodings.

commands.name: clearAcceptedEncodingsOverride
commands.description: Clears accepted encodings set by setAcceptedEncodings

commands.name: canClearBrowserCache
commands.description: Tells whether clearing browser cache is supported.

returns.name: result
returns.description: True if browser cache can be cleared.

commands.name: canClearBrowserCookies
commands.description: Tells whether clearing browser cookies is supported.

returns.name: result
returns.description: True if browser cookies can be cleared.

commands.name: canEmulateNetworkConditions
commands.description: Tells whether emulation of network conditions is supported.

returns.name: result
returns.description: True if emulation of network conditions is supported.

commands.name: clearBrowserCache
commands.description: Clears browser cache.

commands.name: clearBrowserCookies
commands.description: Clears browser cookies.

commands.name: continueInterceptedRequest
commands.description:
Response to Network.requestIntercepted which either modifies the request to continue with any
modifications, or blocks it, or completes it with the provided response bytes. If a network
fetch occurs as a result which encounters a redirect an additional Network.requestIntercepted
event will be sent with the same InterceptionId.
Deprecated, use Fetch.continueRequest, Fetch.fulfillRequest and Fetch.failRequest instead.

parameters.name: errorReason
parameters.description:
If set this causes the request to fail with the given reason. Passing `Aborted` for requests
marked with `isNavigationRequest` also cancels the navigation. Must not be set in response
to an authChallenge.

parameters.name: rawResponse
parameters.description:
If set the requests completes using with the provided base64 encoded raw response, including
HTTP status line and headers etc... Must not be set in response to an authChallenge. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

parameters.name: url
parameters.description:
If set the request url will be modified in a way that's not observable by page. Must not be
set in response to an authChallenge.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description:
If set this allows the request method to be overridden. Must not be set in response to an
authChallenge.

parameters.name: postData
parameters.description: If set this allows postData to be set. Must not be set in response to an authChallenge.

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description:
If set this allows the request headers to be changed. Must not be set in response to an
authChallenge.

parameters.name: authChallengeResponse
parameters.description: Response to a requestIntercepted with an authChallenge. Must not be set otherwise.

commands.name: deleteCookies
commands.description: Deletes browser cookies with matching name and url or domain/path/partitionKey pair.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the cookies to remove.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description:
If specified, deletes all the cookies with the given name where domain and path match
provided URL.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: If specified, deletes only cookies with the exact domain.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: If specified, deletes only cookies with the exact path.

parameters.name: partitionKey
parameters.description:
If specified, deletes only cookies with the the given name and partitionKey where
all partition key attributes match the cookie partition key attribute.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables network tracking, prevents network events from being sent to the client.

commands.name: emulateNetworkConditions
commands.description:
Activates emulation of network conditions. This command is deprecated in favor of the emulateNetworkConditionsByRule
and overrideNetworkState commands, which can be used together to the same effect.

parameters.name: offline
parameters.description: True to emulate internet disconnection.

parameters.name: latency
parameters.description: Minimum latency from request sent to response headers received (ms).

parameters.name: downloadThroughput
parameters.description: Maximal aggregated download throughput (bytes/sec). -1 disables download throttling.

parameters.name: uploadThroughput
parameters.description: Maximal aggregated upload throughput (bytes/sec).  -1 disables upload throttling.

parameters.name: connectionType
parameters.description: Connection type if known.

parameters.name: packetLoss
parameters.description: WebRTC packet loss (percent, 0-100). 0 disables packet loss emulation, 100 drops all the packets.

parameters.name: packetQueueLength
parameters.description: WebRTC packet queue length (packet). 0 removes any queue length limitations.

parameters.name: packetReordering
parameters.description: WebRTC packetReordering feature.

commands.name: emulateNetworkConditionsByRule
commands.description:
Activates emulation of network conditions for individual requests using URL match patterns. Unlike the deprecated
Network.emulateNetworkConditions this method does not affect `navigator` state. Use Network.overrideNetworkState to
explicitly modify `navigator` behavior.

parameters.name: offline
parameters.description: True to emulate internet disconnection.

parameters.name: matchedNetworkConditions
parameters.description:
Configure conditions for matching requests. If multiple entries match a request, the first entry wins.  Global
conditions can be configured by leaving the urlPattern for the conditions empty. These global conditions are
also applied for throttling of p2p connections.

returns.name: ruleIds
returns.description:
An id for each entry in matchedNetworkConditions. The id will be included in the requestWillBeSentExtraInfo for
requests affected by a rule.

commands.name: overrideNetworkState
commands.description: Override the state of navigator.onLine and navigator.connection.

parameters.name: offline
parameters.description: True to emulate internet disconnection.

parameters.name: latency
parameters.description: Minimum latency from request sent to response headers received (ms).

parameters.name: downloadThroughput
parameters.description: Maximal aggregated download throughput (bytes/sec). -1 disables download throttling.

parameters.name: uploadThroughput
parameters.description: Maximal aggregated upload throughput (bytes/sec).  -1 disables upload throttling.

parameters.name: connectionType
parameters.description: Connection type if known.

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enables network tracking, network events will now be delivered to the client.

parameters.name: maxTotalBufferSize
parameters.description:
Buffer size in bytes to use when preserving network payloads (XHRs, etc).
This is the maximum number of bytes that will be collected by this
DevTools session.

parameters.name: maxResourceBufferSize
parameters.description: Per-resource buffer size in bytes to use when preserving network payloads (XHRs, etc).

parameters.name: maxPostDataSize
parameters.description: Longest post body size (in bytes) that would be included in requestWillBeSent notification

parameters.name: reportDirectSocketTraffic
parameters.description: Whether DirectSocket chunk send/receive events should be reported.

parameters.name: enableDurableMessages
parameters.description:
Enable storing response bodies outside of renderer, so that these survive
a cross-process navigation. Requires maxTotalBufferSize to be set.
Currently defaults to false. This field is being deprecated in favor of the dedicated
configureDurableMessages command, due to the possibility of deadlocks when awaiting
Network.enable before issuing Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger.

commands.name: configureDurableMessages
commands.description:
Configures storing response bodies outside of renderer, so that these survive
a cross-process navigation.
If maxTotalBufferSize is not set, durable messages are disabled.

parameters.name: maxTotalBufferSize
parameters.description: Buffer size in bytes to use when preserving network payloads (XHRs, etc).

parameters.name: maxResourceBufferSize
parameters.description: Per-resource buffer size in bytes to use when preserving network payloads (XHRs, etc).

commands.name: getAllCookies
commands.description:
Returns all browser cookies. Depending on the backend support, will return detailed cookie
information in the `cookies` field.
Deprecated. Use Storage.getCookies instead.

commands.name: getCertificate
commands.description: Returns the DER-encoded certificate.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: Origin to get certificate for.

commands.name: getCookies
commands.description:
Returns all browser cookies for the current URL. Depending on the backend support, will return
detailed cookie information in the `cookies` field.

parameters.name: urls
parameters.description:
The list of URLs for which applicable cookies will be fetched.
If not specified, it's assumed to be set to the list containing
the URLs of the page and all of its subframes.

commands.name: getResponseBody
commands.description: Returns content served for the given request.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Identifier of the network request to get content for.

returns.name: base64Encoded
returns.description: True, if content was sent as base64.

commands.name: getRequestPostData
commands.description: Returns post data sent with the request. Returns an error when no data was sent with the request.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Identifier of the network request to get content for.

returns.name: postData
returns.description: Request body string, omitting files from multipart requests

returns.name: base64Encoded
returns.description: True, if content was sent as base64.

commands.name: getResponseBodyForInterception
commands.description: Returns content served for the given currently intercepted request.

parameters.name: interceptionId
parameters.description: Identifier for the intercepted request to get body for.

returns.name: base64Encoded
returns.description: True, if content was sent as base64.

commands.name: takeResponseBodyForInterceptionAsStream
commands.description:
Returns a handle to the stream representing the response body. Note that after this command,
the intercepted request can't be continued as is -- you either need to cancel it or to provide
the response body. The stream only supports sequential read, IO.read will fail if the position
is specified.

commands.name: replayXHR
commands.description:
This method sends a new XMLHttpRequest which is identical to the original one. The following
parameters should be identical: method, url, async, request body, extra headers, withCredentials
attribute, user, password.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Identifier of XHR to replay.

commands.name: searchInResponseBody
commands.description: Searches for given string in response content.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Identifier of the network response to search.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: String to search for.

parameters.name: caseSensitive
parameters.description: If true, search is case sensitive.

parameters.name: isRegex
parameters.description: If true, treats string parameter as regex.

commands.name: setBlockedURLs
commands.description: Blocks URLs from loading.

parameters.name: urlPatterns
parameters.description:
Patterns to match in the order in which they are given. These patterns
also take precedence over any wildcard patterns defined in `urls`.

parameters.name: urls
parameters.description: URL patterns to block. Wildcards ('*') are allowed.

commands.name: setBypassServiceWorker
commands.description: Toggles ignoring of service worker for each request.

parameters.name: bypass
parameters.description: Bypass service worker and load from network.

commands.name: setCacheDisabled
commands.description: Toggles ignoring cache for each request. If `true`, cache will not be used.

parameters.name: cacheDisabled
parameters.description: Cache disabled state.

commands.name: setCookie
commands.description: Sets a cookie with the given cookie data; may overwrite equivalent cookies if they exist.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Cookie name.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Cookie value.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description:
The request-URI to associate with the setting of the cookie. This value can affect the
default domain, path, source port, and source scheme values of the created cookie.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Cookie domain.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Cookie path.

parameters.name: secure
parameters.description: True if cookie is secure.

parameters.name: httpOnly
parameters.description: True if cookie is http-only.

parameters.name: sameSite
parameters.description: Cookie SameSite type.

parameters.name: expires
parameters.description: Cookie expiration date, session cookie if not set

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: Cookie Priority type.

parameters.name: sourceScheme
parameters.description: Cookie source scheme type.

parameters.name: sourcePort
parameters.description:
Cookie source port. Valid values are {-1, [1, 65535]}, -1 indicates an unspecified port.
An unspecified port value allows protocol clients to emulate legacy cookie scope for the port.
This is a temporary ability and it will be removed in the future.

parameters.name: partitionKey
parameters.description: Cookie partition key. If not set, the cookie will be set as not partitioned.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Always set to true. If an error occurs, the response indicates protocol error.

commands.name: setCookies
commands.description: Sets given cookies.

parameters.name: cookies
parameters.description: Cookies to be set.

commands.name: setExtraHTTPHeaders
commands.description: Specifies whether to always send extra HTTP headers with the requests from this page.

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description: Map with extra HTTP headers.

commands.name: setAttachDebugStack
commands.description: Specifies whether to attach a page script stack id in requests

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether to attach a page script stack for debugging purpose.

commands.name: setRequestInterception
commands.description:
Sets the requests to intercept that match the provided patterns and optionally resource types.
Deprecated, please use Fetch.enable instead.

parameters.name: patterns
parameters.description:
Requests matching any of these patterns will be forwarded and wait for the corresponding
continueInterceptedRequest call.

commands.name: setUserAgentOverride
commands.description: Allows overriding user agent with the given string.

parameters.name: userAgent
parameters.description: User agent to use.

parameters.name: acceptLanguage
parameters.description: Browser language to emulate.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: The platform navigator.platform should return.

parameters.name: userAgentMetadata
parameters.description: To be sent in Sec-CH-UA-* headers and returned in navigator.userAgentData

commands.name: streamResourceContent
commands.description:
Enables streaming of the response for the given requestId.
If enabled, the dataReceived event contains the data that was received during streaming.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Identifier of the request to stream.

returns.name: bufferedData
returns.description: Data that has been buffered until streaming is enabled. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

commands.name: getSecurityIsolationStatus
commands.description: Returns information about the COEP/COOP isolation status.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: If no frameId is provided, the status of the target is provided.

commands.name: enableReportingApi
commands.description:
Enables tracking for the Reporting API, events generated by the Reporting API will now be delivered to the client.
Enabling triggers 'reportingApiReportAdded' for all existing reports.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: Whether to enable or disable events for the Reporting API

commands.name: enableDeviceBoundSessions
commands.description: Sets up tracking device bound sessions and fetching of initial set of sessions.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: Whether to enable or disable events.

commands.name: fetchSchemefulSite
commands.description: Fetches the schemeful site for a specific origin.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The URL origin.

commands.name: loadNetworkResource
commands.description: Fetches the resource and returns the content.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description:
Frame id to get the resource for. Mandatory for frame targets, and
should be omitted for worker targets.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL of the resource to get content for.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Options for the request.

commands.name: setCookieControls
commands.description:
Sets Controls for third-party cookie access
Page reload is required before the new cookie behavior will be observed

parameters.name: enableThirdPartyCookieRestriction
parameters.description: Whether 3pc restriction is enabled.

parameters.name: disableThirdPartyCookieMetadata
parameters.description: Whether 3pc grace period exception should be enabled; false by default.

parameters.name: disableThirdPartyCookieHeuristics
parameters.description: Whether 3pc heuristics exceptions should be enabled; false by default.

events.name: dataReceived
events.description: Fired when data chunk was received over the network.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: dataLength
parameters.description: Data chunk length.

parameters.name: encodedDataLength
parameters.description: Actual bytes received (might be less than dataLength for compressed encodings).

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Data that was received. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

events.name: eventSourceMessageReceived
events.description: Fired when EventSource message is received.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: eventName
parameters.description: Message type.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: Message identifier.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Message content.

events.name: loadingFailed
events.description: Fired when HTTP request has failed to load.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Resource type.

parameters.name: errorText
parameters.description: Error message. List of network errors: https://cs.chromium.org/chromium/src/net/base/net_error_list.h

parameters.name: canceled
parameters.description: True if loading was canceled.

parameters.name: blockedReason
parameters.description: The reason why loading was blocked, if any.

parameters.name: corsErrorStatus
parameters.description: The reason why loading was blocked by CORS, if any.

events.name: loadingFinished
events.description: Fired when HTTP request has finished loading.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: encodedDataLength
parameters.description: Total number of bytes received for this request.

events.name: requestIntercepted
events.description:
Details of an intercepted HTTP request, which must be either allowed, blocked, modified or
mocked.
Deprecated, use Fetch.requestPaused instead.

parameters.name: interceptionId
parameters.description:
Each request the page makes will have a unique id, however if any redirects are encountered
while processing that fetch, they will be reported with the same id as the original fetch.
Likewise if HTTP authentication is needed then the same fetch id will be used.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: The id of the frame that initiated the request.

parameters.name: resourceType
parameters.description: How the requested resource will be used.

parameters.name: isNavigationRequest
parameters.description: Whether this is a navigation request, which can abort the navigation completely.

parameters.name: isDownload
parameters.description:
Set if the request is a navigation that will result in a download.
Only present after response is received from the server (i.e. HeadersReceived stage).

parameters.name: redirectUrl
parameters.description: Redirect location, only sent if a redirect was intercepted.

parameters.name: authChallenge
parameters.description:
Details of the Authorization Challenge encountered. If this is set then
continueInterceptedRequest must contain an authChallengeResponse.

parameters.name: responseErrorReason
parameters.description:
Response error if intercepted at response stage or if redirect occurred while intercepting
request.

parameters.name: responseStatusCode
parameters.description:
Response code if intercepted at response stage or if redirect occurred while intercepting
request or auth retry occurred.

parameters.name: responseHeaders
parameters.description:
Response headers if intercepted at the response stage or if redirect occurred while
intercepting request or auth retry occurred.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description:
If the intercepted request had a corresponding requestWillBeSent event fired for it, then
this requestId will be the same as the requestId present in the requestWillBeSent event.

events.name: requestServedFromCache
events.description: Fired if request ended up loading from cache.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

events.name: requestWillBeSent
events.description: Fired when page is about to send HTTP request.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: loaderId
parameters.description: Loader identifier. Empty string if the request is fetched from worker.

parameters.name: documentURL
parameters.description: URL of the document this request is loaded for.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Request data.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: wallTime
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: initiator
parameters.description: Request initiator.

parameters.name: redirectHasExtraInfo
parameters.description:
In the case that redirectResponse is populated, this flag indicates whether
requestWillBeSentExtraInfo and responseReceivedExtraInfo events will be or were emitted
for the request which was just redirected.

parameters.name: redirectResponse
parameters.description: Redirect response data.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of this resource.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Frame identifier.

parameters.name: hasUserGesture
parameters.description: Whether the request is initiated by a user gesture. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: renderBlockingBehavior
parameters.description: The render-blocking behavior of the request.

events.name: resourceChangedPriority
events.description: Fired when resource loading priority is changed

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: newPriority
parameters.description: New priority

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

events.name: signedExchangeReceived
events.description: Fired when a signed exchange was received over the network

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: info
parameters.description: Information about the signed exchange response.

events.name: responseReceived
events.description: Fired when HTTP response is available.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: loaderId
parameters.description: Loader identifier. Empty string if the request is fetched from worker.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Resource type.

parameters.name: response
parameters.description: Response data.

parameters.name: hasExtraInfo
parameters.description:
Indicates whether requestWillBeSentExtraInfo and responseReceivedExtraInfo events will be
or were emitted for this request.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Frame identifier.

events.name: webSocketClosed
events.description: Fired when WebSocket is closed.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

events.name: webSocketCreated
events.description: Fired upon WebSocket creation.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: WebSocket request URL.

parameters.name: initiator
parameters.description: Request initiator.

events.name: webSocketFrameError
events.description: Fired when WebSocket message error occurs.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: errorMessage
parameters.description: WebSocket error message.

events.name: webSocketFrameReceived
events.description: Fired when WebSocket message is received.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: response
parameters.description: WebSocket response data.

events.name: webSocketFrameSent
events.description: Fired when WebSocket message is sent.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: response
parameters.description: WebSocket response data.

events.name: webSocketHandshakeResponseReceived
events.description: Fired when WebSocket handshake response becomes available.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: response
parameters.description: WebSocket response data.

events.name: webSocketWillSendHandshakeRequest
events.description: Fired when WebSocket is about to initiate handshake.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: wallTime
parameters.description: UTC Timestamp.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: WebSocket request data.

events.name: webTransportCreated
events.description: Fired upon WebTransport creation.

parameters.name: transportId
parameters.description: WebTransport identifier.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: WebTransport request URL.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: initiator
parameters.description: Request initiator.

events.name: webTransportConnectionEstablished
events.description: Fired when WebTransport handshake is finished.

parameters.name: transportId
parameters.description: WebTransport identifier.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

events.name: webTransportClosed
events.description: Fired when WebTransport is disposed.

parameters.name: transportId
parameters.description: WebTransport identifier.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

events.name: directTCPSocketCreated
events.description: Fired upon direct_socket.TCPSocket creation.

parameters.name: remotePort
parameters.description: Unsigned int 16.

events.name: directTCPSocketOpened
events.description: Fired when direct_socket.TCPSocket connection is opened.

parameters.name: remotePort
parameters.description: Expected to be unsigned integer.

parameters.name: localPort
parameters.description: Expected to be unsigned integer.

events.name: directTCPSocketAborted
events.description: Fired when direct_socket.TCPSocket is aborted.

events.name: directTCPSocketClosed
events.description: Fired when direct_socket.TCPSocket is closed.

events.name: directTCPSocketChunkSent
events.description: Fired when data is sent to tcp direct socket stream.

events.name: directTCPSocketChunkReceived
events.description: Fired when data is received from tcp direct socket stream.

events.name: directUDPSocketCreated
events.description: Fired upon direct_socket.UDPSocket creation.

events.name: directUDPSocketOpened
events.description: Fired when direct_socket.UDPSocket connection is opened.

parameters.name: localPort
parameters.description: Expected to be unsigned integer.

parameters.name: remotePort
parameters.description: Expected to be unsigned integer.

events.name: directUDPSocketAborted
events.description: Fired when direct_socket.UDPSocket is aborted.

events.name: directUDPSocketClosed
events.description: Fired when direct_socket.UDPSocket is closed.

events.name: directUDPSocketChunkSent
events.description: Fired when message is sent to udp direct socket stream.

events.name: directUDPSocketChunkReceived
events.description: Fired when message is received from udp direct socket stream.

events.name: requestWillBeSentExtraInfo
events.description:
Fired when additional information about a requestWillBeSent event is available from the
network stack. Not every requestWillBeSent event will have an additional
requestWillBeSentExtraInfo fired for it, and there is no guarantee whether requestWillBeSent
or requestWillBeSentExtraInfo will be fired first for the same request.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier. Used to match this information to an existing requestWillBeSent event.

parameters.name: associatedCookies
parameters.description:
A list of cookies potentially associated to the requested URL. This includes both cookies sent with
the request and the ones not sent; the latter are distinguished by having blockedReasons field set.

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description: Raw request headers as they will be sent over the wire.

parameters.name: connectTiming
parameters.description: Connection timing information for the request.

parameters.name: deviceBoundSessionUsages
parameters.description: How the request site's device bound sessions were used during this request.

parameters.name: clientSecurityState
parameters.description: The client security state set for the request.

parameters.name: siteHasCookieInOtherPartition
parameters.description: Whether the site has partitioned cookies stored in a partition different than the current one.

parameters.name: appliedNetworkConditionsId
parameters.description:
The network conditions id if this request was affected by network conditions configured via
emulateNetworkConditionsByRule.

events.name: responseReceivedExtraInfo
events.description:
Fired when additional information about a responseReceived event is available from the network
stack. Not every responseReceived event will have an additional responseReceivedExtraInfo for
it, and responseReceivedExtraInfo may be fired before or after responseReceived.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier. Used to match this information to another responseReceived event.

parameters.name: blockedCookies
parameters.description:
A list of cookies which were not stored from the response along with the corresponding
reasons for blocking. The cookies here may not be valid due to syntax errors, which
are represented by the invalid cookie line string instead of a proper cookie.

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description:
Raw response headers as they were received over the wire.
Duplicate headers in the response are represented as a single key with their values
concatentated using `\n` as the separator.
See also `headersText` that contains verbatim text for HTTP/1.*.

parameters.name: resourceIPAddressSpace
parameters.description:
The IP address space of the resource. The address space can only be determined once the transport
established the connection, so we can't send it in `requestWillBeSentExtraInfo`.

parameters.name: statusCode
parameters.description:
The status code of the response. This is useful in cases the request failed and no responseReceived
event is triggered, which is the case for, e.g., CORS errors. This is also the correct status code
for cached requests, where the status in responseReceived is a 200 and this will be 304.

parameters.name: headersText
parameters.description:
Raw response header text as it was received over the wire. The raw text may not always be
available, such as in the case of HTTP/2 or QUIC.

parameters.name: cookiePartitionKey
parameters.description:
The cookie partition key that will be used to store partitioned cookies set in this response.
Only sent when partitioned cookies are enabled.

parameters.name: cookiePartitionKeyOpaque
parameters.description: True if partitioned cookies are enabled, but the partition key is not serializable to string.

parameters.name: exemptedCookies
parameters.description:
A list of cookies which should have been blocked by 3PCD but are exempted and stored from
the response with the corresponding reason.

events.name: responseReceivedEarlyHints
events.description:
Fired when 103 Early Hints headers is received in addition to the common response.
Not every responseReceived event will have an responseReceivedEarlyHints fired.
Only one responseReceivedEarlyHints may be fired for eached responseReceived event.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier. Used to match this information to another responseReceived event.

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description:
Raw response headers as they were received over the wire.
Duplicate headers in the response are represented as a single key with their values
concatentated using `\n` as the separator.
See also `headersText` that contains verbatim text for HTTP/1.*.

events.name: trustTokenOperationDone
events.description:
Fired exactly once for each Trust Token operation. Depending on
the type of the operation and whether the operation succeeded or
failed, the event is fired before the corresponding request was sent
or after the response was received.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Detailed success or error status of the operation.
'AlreadyExists' also signifies a successful operation, as the result
of the operation already exists und thus, the operation was abort
preemptively (e.g. a cache hit).

parameters.name: topLevelOrigin
parameters.description: Top level origin. The context in which the operation was attempted.

parameters.name: issuerOrigin
parameters.description: Origin of the issuer in case of a "Issuance" or "Redemption" operation.

parameters.name: issuedTokenCount
parameters.description: The number of obtained Trust Tokens on a successful "Issuance" operation.

events.name: policyUpdated
events.description: Fired once security policy has been updated.

events.name: reportingApiReportAdded
events.description:
Is sent whenever a new report is added.
And after 'enableReportingApi' for all existing reports.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: Origin of the document(s) which configured the endpoints.

events.name: deviceBoundSessionsAdded
events.description: Triggered when the initial set of device bound sessions is added.

parameters.name: sessions
parameters.description: The device bound sessions.

events.name: deviceBoundSessionEventOccurred
events.description: Triggered when a device bound session event occurs.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: A unique identifier for this session event.

parameters.name: site
parameters.description: The site this session event is associated with.

parameters.name: succeeded
parameters.description: Whether this event was considered successful.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description:
The session ID this event is associated with. May not be populated for
failed events.

parameters.name: creationEventDetails
parameters.description: The below are the different session event type details. Exactly one is populated.
